Researcher & Science Communicator

Neha Gour

PhD Candidate · George Mason University

Working at the intersection of AI infrastructure, climate and health communication — studying how emerging technologies shape our most pressing human challenges.

Research

Publications

✦ Recent Publication

Health implications of the rapid rise of data centers in Virginia: An exploratory assessment

Gour, N., Ortiz, L., Maibach, E.

Frontiers in Climate, 8, 1648912 · 2026

As data centers expand rapidly across Virginia to support AI infrastructure, this study offers an exploratory assessment of the public health implications — examining energy demand, air quality, and community-level health risks associated with this growth.
View Paper → Cite

5 ways data centers endanger their local communities and the country as a whole

Every internet search, streamed video and AI-generated response depends on a data center. But what does rapid data center expansion mean for the health of people living next to them?

I welcome correspondence from researchers, journalists, policymakers, and anyone working at the intersection of technology, climate, and health. Feel free to reach out for collaborations, media inquiries, or speaking engagements.
